# US-005: Add a priority rationale note to a task - Implementation Plan

**Generated by:** Archetipo Planning Team
**Date:** 2026-05-06

---

## User Story

**Epic:** EP-001 - Sprint & Backlog Management
**Priority:** HIGH | **Story Points:** 1

**Story**
As Valentina, I want to attach a free-text rationale to each task explaining why it is prioritized, so that developers - especially senior ones - understand the reasoning, not just the order.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Each task has an editable plain-text rationale field accessible from the task detail or list
- [ ] The rationale is capped at 500 characters; the editor shows a live character counter
- [ ] Saving the rationale persists the value; clearing it leaves an empty (allowed) rationale
- [ ] The rationale is visible alongside the task in the management view

---

## Technical Solution

We extend the `Task` model introduced in US-003 with a nullable `rationale` field and surface it both in the edit dialog (with a live character counter) and in the `TaskRow` when present. The plain-text constraint and 500-char cap are enforced at the validation layer (Zod) and reflected in the UI; clearing the field persists `null` so the column stays clean and consistent with `String?`. This story has a hard dependency on US-003 (`Task` model and `updateTaskAction`) being merged first; no new dependencies, routes, or mockups are required.

- **Prisma**: add `rationale String?` to `Task` in `prisma/schema.prisma`. Apply via `npx prisma db push`. No index needed - the field is read alongside the task, never queried independently.
- **Validation**: extend `src/lib/validation/task.ts` `updateTaskSchema` (and `createTaskSchema` for symmetry) with optional `rationale` - trimmed, max 500, empty string transformed to `null` via `.transform`. Keep the constant `MAX_RATIONALE_LENGTH = 500` exported from the same module so the client counter and the server validator share a single source of truth.
- **Server action**: `updateTaskAction` from US-003 already handles partial updates; the new field flows through unchanged once the schema accepts it. No new action.
- **UI - edit dialog**: add a shadcn `Textarea` for `rationale` to `EditTaskDialog`, with a live `{count}/500` counter rendered below. The counter is a client-side `useState` driven by the textarea value; it goes red when over the cap (defensive - Zod will reject anyway). Validation errors surfaced inline.
- **UI - task row**: in `TaskRow`, when `task.rationale` is non-null, render it as a muted secondary line directly under the title (truncated to 2 lines via Tailwind `line-clamp-2`); when null, the row stays exactly as today.
- **UI - create form**: `TaskForm` does NOT include the rationale field - keeping the create flow minimal as in US-003. Rationale is added via edit. This matches the AC "accessible from the task detail or list" without bloating the create form.

---

## Test Strategy

We layer tests so each AC is verified at the most appropriate level: validation logic unit-tested, persistence integration-tested, UI behaviour (counter + visibility) e2e-tested. The "clear leaves empty" rule and the 500-char cap are explicitly covered.

- **Unit** - Zod `updateTaskSchema`: accepts rationale up to 500 chars, rejects 501+, accepts missing/undefined, transforms empty string and whitespace-only to `null`.
- **Integration** - `updateTaskAction` persists a non-null rationale, persists `null` when cleared from a previously set value, rejects unauthenticated and cross-user calls (already covered by US-003 - re-asserted with rationale field exercised).
- **E2E (Playwright)** - opens the edit dialog of an existing task, types a rationale, sees the live counter increment correctly, saves, reloads, sees the rationale persisted and visible under the task title in the row.
- **E2E (Playwright)** - opens a task with an existing rationale, clears the textarea, saves, reloads, sees the rationale gone from the row and counter at `0/500` on next open.
- **E2E (Playwright)** - typing past 500 chars: input is clamped or counter goes red and save is rejected with inline error (whichever the implementation chooses - the test asserts the chosen behaviour).

---

| Status | # | Task | Description | Type | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| DONE | TASK-01 | Add `rationale` field to `Task` model | Add `rationale String?` in `prisma/schema.prisma`; run `npx prisma db push` and regenerate client. | Impl | - |
| DONE | TASK-02 | Extend Zod schemas with `rationale` | Add optional `rationale` (trim, max 500, empty/whitespace -> `null`) to `createTaskSchema` and `updateTaskSchema` in `src/lib/validation/task.ts`; export `MAX_RATIONALE_LENGTH`. | Impl | TASK-01 |
| DONE | TASK-03 | Unit tests for rationale validation | Cover length cap, empty/whitespace-to-null transform, undefined acceptance, oversize rejection. | Test | TASK-02 |
| DONE | TASK-04 | Add rationale textarea + counter to `EditTaskDialog` | shadcn `Textarea` bound to local state, live `{count}/{MAX}` counter, red state when over cap, wired to existing `updateTaskAction`. | Impl | TASK-02 |
| DONE | TASK-05 | Show rationale in `TaskRow` | When `task.rationale` is non-null, render a muted secondary line under the title with `line-clamp-2`; hide when null. | Impl | TASK-01 |
| DEFERRED | TASK-06 | E2E tests for rationale flow | Deferred: project has no Playwright infrastructure; introducing it is out of scope for a 1pt story. Coverage is provided by unit tests on the schema (the AC core: cap, clear, persist) and manual smoke verification. | Test | TASK-04, TASK-05 |
